THIS JUDGMENT WAS REACHED BY STIPULATION. PLAINTIFF, YELANA YATSKAR SHALL HAVE RESTITUTION AND POSSESSION OF 3585-18TH STREET. THERE WILL BE A STAY OF EXECUTION THROUGH 7-10-95, 5PM. DEFENDANTS MICHAEL MULL AND TODD SEITZER WILL BE GIVEN A CREDIT OF $1300 AGAINST $2,981. THE LATTER AMOUNT, $2,981 REPRESENTS RENT AND DAMAGES THROUGH 7-10-95. IF DEFENDANTS, TURN IN THEIR KEYS TO PLAINTIFF BY 7-10-85, THEY WILL BE GIVEN A CREDIT OF $650 AGAINST THIS JUDGMENT. IF DEFENDANTS VACATE BY 6-30-95, $296 ADDITIONAL CREDIT WILL BE ALLOWED AGAINST THE JUDGMENT. EACH SIDE SHALL BEAR ITS OWN COSTS. THERE WILL NOT BE ANY FURTHER STAYS OF EXECUTION. EACH SIDE SHALL RELEASE ALL CLAIMS AGAINST EACH OTHER. THE PROVISIONS OF 1542, A GENERAL RELEASE, ARE IN EFFECT. ANY POSSESSIONS LEFT ON THE PREMISES AFTER 7-10-95, 5PM BECOME THE PROPERTY OF THE OWNER. THERE AREN'T ANY OTHER ADULTS IN POSSESSION ON THE PREMISES.
